titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Roy and Clara Creighton Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Alice Yafeh and Martha Lund Smalley

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday July 24, 2020 at 22:05

did.unittitle: Roy and Clara Creighton Papers
did.abstract: Consisting primarily of correspondence between the Creightons and family members in the U.S., this collection provides valuable detailed information about Roy Creighton's work, the life of a typical missionary wife, and the complications of raising a family on the mission field. Roy Creighton was an American architect who supervised the construction of buildings on the mission field. He served under the YMCA and Presbyterian Board in China, at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon, and Robert College in Turkey. He also taught at the Engineering School of the College of Chinese Studies. Clara Creighton was a missionary wife and homemaker.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Roy and Clara Creighton Papers (RG 177), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: After graduation from Harvard, Roy went to Hangzhou (Hangchow), China as a Building Supervisor for the YMCA
Clara came out to China and they were married at Kuling
Roy supervised hospital construction at Boone University, Wuchang
Roy worked for Architects Bureau of the Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A. in Shanghai
Following furlough in the U.S., the Creightons when to Beirut, Lebanon (Syria) where Roy supervised construction at the American University of Beirut.
Following furlough in the U.S., the Creightons went to Istanbul, Turkey, where Roy supervised the building of a library for Robert College.

Following furlough in the U.S., the Creightons returned to China, based in Beijing (Peking). Roy taught at Engineering School of the College of Chinese Studies and traveled to supervise construction of Presbyterian Mission buildings.
Clara and two younger children evacuated to U.S. due to war conditions; Roy remained in Beijing (Peking).
Roy working at New York office of Presbyterian Mission Board
Roy to Shanghai to rehabilitate mission properties
Roy and Clara to Shanghai, evacuated to Hong Kong
Roy and Clara in New York, Presbyterian Board offices

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many of the letters in this collection were published by Roger L. Creighton as a book entitled "84 Randall Ave. The Lives of Roy and Clara Creighton; Letters of 1908-1978" (in YDSL cataloged collection).

These papers provide valuable insight into the work of a missionary architect and building supervisor who worked primarily in China, first for the YMCA and later for the Presbyterian Mission Board. The letters are primarily from the Creightons to their family members in the U.S., at first primarily their parents and siblings, but later their children. Periodic circular letters by the Creightons provide succinct summaries of their work. The letters of Clara Creighton from China provide an excellent record of the daily life of a woman missionary who was primarily a mother and homemaker, including documentation of the social life of mainline Protestant, city-based missionaries.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Place names were modernized in the description, with the name originally used in the collection material or in an older version of the finding aid in parenthesis: e.g. "Beijing (Peking)" or "Benin (Dahomey)".

did.unittitle: Correspondence
RLC and CLC to each other and to others; in U.S.
RLC to CLC; in U.S., on journey to China
Roy to Clara; from YMCA Hangzhou (Hangchow) and YMCA Hankou (Hankow); includes Boone University, Wuchang, 6th Annual Commencement program
Roy to Clara; from Hankou (Hankow)
Primarily from Roy in Hankou (Hankow); arrangements for marriage of Roy and Clara
RLC, CLC to various; journey of CLC to China; marriage in Kuling
RLC, CLC to various; from Kuling
Roy, Clara to various; from Hankou (Hankow)
Primarily Roy, Clara to various; from Hankou (Hankow); birth of son (Linn)
Roy, Clara to various; from Hankou (Hankow), Kuling
Roy, Clara to various; from Hankou (Hankow), Kuling
Roy, Clara to various; from Hankou (Hankow)
Roy, Clara to various; from Shanghai, from Hangzhou (Hangchow)
Roy, Clara to various; from Hangzhou (Hangchow)
Return to U.S. on furlough
Furlough in United States; return to Hangzhou (Hangchow); birth of daughter (Marjory); passport of Roy Creighton
RLC, CLC to various, from Shanghai; re. death of daughter
RLC, CLC to various; from Kuling, Shanghai; condolences re. death of daughter.
RLC, CLC to various; from Shanghai; birth of son (Roger)
RLC, CLC to various; from Shanghai; circular letter (Feb. 11) gives overview of work with Mission Architects Bureau, news re. bandits
Boy, Clara Creighton to various; from Beidaihe (Peitaho), Beijing (Peking), Shanghai; re: birth of daughter (Phyllis)

RLC, CLC to various; from Shanghai; includes pamphlet "Missions and Architecture - Residences" by Charles A. Gunn and Roy L. Creighton, published by the Architects Bureau of the Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A.
RLC, CLC to various, from Shanghai; incl. circular letter 1926 Jan 15 re. political events, anti-Christian movement
RLC, CLC to various; from U.S., enroute to Syria, from American University of Beirut
RLC, CLC to various; from Beirut
RLC, CLC to various; from Beirut
RLC, CLC to various; from U.S., enroute to Turkey, from Robert College, Istanbul; letter from Linn to grandfather
RLC, CLC to various; from Robert College, Istanbul
Roy, Clara to various; from U.S., enroute to China; from Hangzhou (Hangchow)
Roy, Clara, children to various; from Beijing (Peking), Beidaihe (Petaiho)

Roy Creighton, Clara Creighton to various; from Beijing (Peking), Beidaihe (Peitaiho)

Roy, Clara, to/from Linn and others; from Beijing (Peking)
Roy, Clara, to/from Linn and others; from Beijing (Peking)
Roy Creighton, Clara Creighton, to and from children and others; from Beijing (Peking), Beidaihe (Peitaiho)
Roy, Clara to/from children and others; from Beijing (Peking); newspaper clippings re. Sino-Japanese war
Roy, Clara to/from children and others; from Beijing (Peking), return to U.S.
Roy, Clara to/from children and others; from U.S., return to Beijing (Peking)
Roy, Clara to/from children and others; from Beijing (Peking)
Roy, Clara to/from children and others; from Beijing (Peking)
Roy Creighton to family; from Beijing (Peking); includes newspaper article by Rosamund Frame, text of a talk on missionary children entitled "Children of Two Cultures"
Roy Creighton to family; from Beijing (Peking)
RLC to/from family; family to/from each other; RLC a war prisoner of Japanese following Dec. 7.
Family in U.S. to RLC in internment; mimeographed China Bulletins from Presbyterian Foreign Mission Board re. interned missionaries
To/from family members in U.S.and RLC in internment; mimeographed China Bulletin from Presbyterian Foreign Mission Board re. interned missionaries
To/from various family members in U.S.and RLC in internment; mimeographed China Bulletins from Presbyterian Foreign Mission Board re. interned missionaries

RLC, CLC to/from family members and Presbyterian church women; from Hong Kong, from Thailand
RLC, CLC to/from family members and Presbyterian church women; from Hong Kong, from Thailand
